title: I.Sicily inscription 000855

editionAuthor.title: Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus
editionAuthor.author: Kaibel
editionAuthor.citation: G. Kaibel, Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus, Inscriptiones Graecae consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae Editae. Volumen XIV., XIV (Berlin: Georgius Reimerus, 1890)

support: Described by Franz as a small altar, by Kaibel as a cippus; dimensions are recorded in the inventory ('cippo sepolcrale di tufo calcare frammentato')

layout.p: Text centred on the upper part of the stone.

handNote.lettering: Lunate epsilon and sigma, uncial omega; use of interpuncts.

provenanceFound._: First recorded by Franz in CIG 3; autopsied by Kaibel in the Siracusa museum and recorded in the inventory of 1885; but not included in Orsi's Taccuini of 1888-1889, and seemingly not observed since.

museum.name: Museo Archeologico Regionale Paolo Orsi
museum.description: An outstanding collection of archaeological material. The collection ranges from Siracusa and the surrounding territory (Megara Hyblaia, Akrai, Kasmenai etc) to encompass all of eastern Sicily, reaching as far as Gela and Agrigento. The ground floor covers prehistory through to the end of the Classical period. Upstairs includes Hellenistic and Roman Syracuse and a display of material from the catacombs. A gallery displaying material from Hellenistic eastern Sicily is in preparation.
